title: dalbergia bintuluensis
intro: Dalbergia bintuluensis, also known as Bintulu padauk, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. It is native to Borneo and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, headache, and skin infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial and fungal infections. Traditionally, the herbal preparation of Dalbergia bintuluensis is made by boiling the roots and leaves in water, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application.
